header.title: Help & Documentation
header.subtitle: Learn how to use the Kaiord Editor to create and manage structured workout files

creating.heading: Creating a Workout
creating.step1: Click "Create New Workout" on the welcome screen
creating.step2: Enter a workout name and select a sport (cycling, running, swimming)
creating.step3: Add workout steps by clicking "Add Step"
creating.step4: Configure duration (time, distance, or open) for each step
creating.step5: Set target intensity (power, heart rate, pace, or cadence)
creating.step6: Save your workout using Ctrl+S (Cmd+S on Mac)

loading.heading: Loading a Workout
loading.step1: Click "Load Workout" or drag and drop a file
loading.step2: Supported formats: KRD, FIT, TCX, ZWO
loading.step3: The workout will load and display all steps
loading.step4: Edit any step by clicking on it

organizing.heading: Organizing Steps
organizing.step1: Drag and drop steps to reorder them
organizing.step2: Use Alt+Up/Down to move selected steps
organizing.step3: Group steps into repetition blocks with Ctrl+G (Cmd+G on Mac)
organizing.step4: Ungroup blocks with Ctrl+Shift+G (Cmd+Shift+G on Mac)
organizing.step5: Copy steps with Ctrl+C and paste with Ctrl+V

sweetSpot.title: Sweet Spot Intervals
sweetSpot.description: Classic sweet spot training with 3x10 minute intervals at 88-93% FTP
sweetSpot.step2: 3x (10 min at 88-93% FTP, 5 min recovery at 50% FTP)

tempo.title: Tempo Run
tempo.description: Sustained tempo effort to build aerobic endurance

formats.a: The editor supports KRD (native format), FIT (Garmin), TCX (Training Center XML), and ZWO (Zwift) formats for both import and export.

block.q: How do I create a repetition block?
block.a: Select multiple steps by clicking while holding Shift or Ctrl, then press Ctrl+G (Cmd+G on Mac) or click the 'Create Block' button. You can also drag steps into an existing block.

offline.a: Yes! The editor works offline once loaded. Your workouts are saved in your browser's local storage and will be available even without an internet connection.

zones.a: Training zones are intensity ranges based on your FTP (power) or maximum heart rate. Configure your zones in the Profile Manager to see zone-based targets in your workouts.

export.q: How do I export my workout?
export.a: Click the 'Save' button and select your desired export format (KRD, FIT, TCX, or ZWO). The file will be downloaded to your device.

undo.a: Yes! Use Ctrl+Z (Cmd+Z on Mac) to undo and Ctrl+Y (Cmd+Y on Mac) to redo. The editor maintains a full history of your changes.

durations.q: What's the difference between duration types?
durations.a: Time-based durations use minutes/seconds, distance-based use meters/kilometers, and open durations continue until you manually advance (lap button press).

library.q: How do I save workouts to my library?
library.a: Click the 'Save to Library' button to store your workout locally. You can add tags and notes for easy organization and retrieval.
